message:
feat(io_internal_abi): wide-FILE vtable routes to wide stream hooks

_IO_wfile_jumps was previously aliased to the narrow trampolines
(trampoline_finish/overflow/underflow/etc.), with the comment
"wide dispatch is TODO". That meant any libc consumer that
discovered a wide-oriented FILE* and dispatched through its vtable
silently fell through the narrow byte path — getwc/putwc/fwide
behavior was undefined-by-default rather than handled.

Add 12 wide-specific trampolines that forward to the existing
glibc wide hooks already linked by frankenlibc-abi:

- trampoline_wfinish    -> _IO_wdefault_finish
- trampoline_woverflow  -> _IO_wfile_overflow   (wchar in u32 arg)
- trampoline_wunderflow -> _IO_wfile_underflow
- trampoline_wuflow     -> _IO_wdefault_uflow
- trampoline_wpbackfail -> _IO_wdefault_pbackfail
- trampoline_wxsputn    -> _IO_wfile_xsputn
- trampoline_wxsgetn    -> _IO_wdefault_xsgetn
- trampoline_wseekoff   -> _IO_wfile_seekoff
- trampoline_wseekpos   -> _IO_wfile_seekoff(SEEK_SET)
- trampoline_wsetbuf    -> _IO_wsetb (computes end pointer from buf+size)
- trampoline_wsync      -> _IO_wfile_sync
- trampoline_wdoallocate-> _IO_wdefault_doallocate

Wire the wide vtable's __finish/__overflow/__underflow/__uflow/
__pbackfail/__xsputn/__xsgetn/__seekoff/__seekpos/__setbuf/__sync/
__doallocate slots to the new wide trampolines. Update the
doc-comment from "same trampolines as _IO_file_jumps (wide dispatch
is TODO)" to "routes wide slots to the wide FILE hooks and raw fd
slots to the same low-level byte syscalls as regular files" — the
__read/__write slots intentionally stay shared with the narrow
table because the underlying syscalls (read(2)/write(2)) are
byte-stream operations that don't care about wide/narrow framing.

Add wide_file_jump_table_uses_wide_slots covering the divergence
contract:

- Wide __overflow/underflow/uflow/pbackfail/xsputn/xsgetn invoked
  on a NULL FILE* return -1 / 0 (matching the wide hooks' error
  path) — proves the slots actually dispatch through the wide
  trampolines, not the narrow ones (narrow trampolines would have
  different return shapes on NULL).
- assert_ne! on __overflow/underflow/xsputn function pointers
  between narrow and wide tables — proves they're different
  trampolines, not aliased.
- assert_eq! on __read/__write — proves the raw fd slots remain
  intentionally shared.

Existing narrow-vtable behavior is unchanged; this only fills in
the previously-stubbed wide table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
